refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable achieving the customer-defined cycle time target for the load phase of the load→travel→dump cycle.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR combined the entire load→travel→dump cycle into one requirement, which includes multiple distinct phases. To be atomic and solution-neutral, the FR is split into sub-FRs each addressing a single phase of the cycle. This refinement focuses on the load phase as one atomic functional requirement.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the cycle time for the load phase under the agreed duty cycle and site conditions, verifying that the 95th percentile cycle time (p95) is less than or equal to the customer-defined target.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall enable achieving the customer-defined cycle time target for the load phase of the load→travel→dump cycle.
The NGHE solution shall enable achieving the customer-defined cycle time target for the travel phase of the load→travel→dump cycle.
The NGHE solution shall enable achieving the customer-defined cycle time target for the dump phase of the load→travel→dump cycle.

candidate_dps: Optimize load phase operational sequence to minimize idle and transition times
Implement adaptive control of load equipment to dynamically adjust to site conditions
Design load equipment with capacity and speed parameters aligned to cycle time targets
Integrate real-time monitoring and feedback to adjust load phase parameters
Standardize load phase procedures and training to reduce variability
selected_dp.selected_dp: Optimize load phase operational sequence to minimize idle and transition times

potential_sources: Interdependence between load phase and travel phase timing
Variability in site conditions affecting load equipment performance
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP focuses on the operational sequence optimization, which is logically independent from other phases and equipment design, supporting modularity and minimizing coupling.

assumptions_risks: Cycle time target is achievable through operational improvements alone
Site conditions and duty cycle remain within expected ranges
Operational policies allow for sequence optimization without compromising safety
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly including adaptive control mechanisms as a secondary DP to handle variability in site conditions and maintain cycle time targets under changing environments.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses the core operational logic and control algorithms for the system, including optimization of load phase sequences.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Outputs operational status and performance metrics related to load phase optimization.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives commands and scheduling inputs to adjust load phase operations.

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as optimization logic is central to NGHE Solution; minimal diffusion as interfaces handle external communication.
Partial containment possible but Vehicle module is broader and includes hardware control, leading to moderate diffusion.
Teleoperation focuses on remote control, less related to load phase optimization, causing high diffusion.
Safety Services focus on safety alerts and monitoring, not operational sequence optimization, resulting in poor containment and high diffusion.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most specific and appropriate boundary to contain the DP 'Optimize load phase operational sequence' because it directly manages operational logic and sequencing, minimizing diffusion and maximizing cohesion.
containment_risks: Potential overlap with Vehicle module if hardware constraints affect optimization logic.
Interfaces with Tasking and Telemetry must be well-defined to avoid unintended coupling.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP 'Optimize load phase operational sequence to minimize idle and transition times' directly addresses the FR of achieving the customer-defined cycle time target for the load phase. The DP logically decomposes the FR by focusing on operational sequence optimization to meet the cycle time acceptance criteria.

diffusion_risks: Potential overlap with Vehicle module if hardware constraints affect optimization logic.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module has high cohesion and minimal diffusion, making it the most appropriate IM boundary for the DP. Other modules show higher diffusion scores and lower containment, supporting this allocation. Interfaces with Tasking and Telemetry are well defined to handle external communication, minimizing diffusion risk.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible through measuring cycle time p95 against the CT_target under agreed duty cycle and site conditions, as well as monitoring operational policies and safety plans.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The ownership of the DP by the NGHE Solution module is clear and well justified, with explicit interfaces supporting control and telemetry functions.

recommended_revisions: Clarify and document the interface contracts between NGHE Solution and Vehicle module to manage potential overlap and hardware constraints.
Ensure that Tasking and Telemetry interfaces are strictly controlled to prevent unintended coupling and maintain modularity.

decision.justification: Although all gates have passed, there are identified risks related to potential overlap with the Vehicle module and the need to clarify interface contracts to manage hardware constraints and maintain modularity. Given the high safety criticality, it is prudent to revise the current level to address these interface and diffusion concerns before proceeding with further decomposition.

revise.why: To clarify and document interface contracts between NGHE Solution and Vehicle modules, and to ensure strict control of Tasking and Telemetry interfaces to prevent unintended coupling and maintain modularity.

assumptions: The DP 'Optimize load phase operational sequence' sufficiently addresses the FR for cycle time target.
The NGHE Solution module is the appropriate IM boundary for the DP based on cohesion and diffusion analysis.
open_risks: Potential overlap with Vehicle module due to hardware constraints affecting optimization logic.
Risk of unintended coupling through Tasking and Telemetry interfaces.
evidence_needed: Detailed interface contracts between NGHE Solution and Vehicle modules.
Verification data on cycle time p95 under operational conditions.
Documentation of interface control measures for Tasking and Telemetry modules.
